description_long: This is a long, arched-roof cowshed with a corrugated reddish-brown metal roof and white vertical siding on the end walls. The front features a wide double-door entrance made of wood, flanked by two rectangular windows with visible frames. The structure is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, and is designed for practical agricultural use. The rear wall has two additional windows but no doors, while the sides are solid and uninterrupted. The roof's curvature and the overall elongated form suggest efficient use of space and easy runoff of rainwater. The materials and construction indicate durability and suitability for housing livestock in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A long, arched-roof cowshed with a reddish-brown metal roof, white end walls, and a wide wooden double-door entrance flanked by windows. The structure is utilitarian and built for durability.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide double wooden doors and two rectangular windows on a white end wall.
description_view_1: Side view displays the full length of the arched reddish-brown roof with the entrance doors just visible at one end.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a white end wall with two rectangular windows and no doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the other side, showing the continuous arched roof and the rear wall's windows.
attribute_door_style: double wooden plank doors, outward opening
attribute_window_presence: four rectangular windows, two on each end wall
